gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Applied by analogy: plain water GI = 0, sentinel value 1 used per Veritas convention.

gi_source.source_match_notes: This item is a non-food reusable drinkware product containing no carbohydrates and no glycemic substance. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule) because the item contains no glycemic carbohydrates whatsoever. The analogous reference is plain water (GI effectively 0) from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables, both approved sources. gi_category is low by convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero. This is the most defensible approved-source anchor available for a non-food item with zero carbohydrate content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Alley Eco Cup (500 ml) is a branded reusable drinkware product sold by The Alley, a Taiwanese bubble tea and specialty beverage chain with outlets in Singapore including The Alley at Funan Mall. This item is merchandise — a physical reusable cup with a 500 ml capacity — and is not a consumable food or beverage. As a non-food item, it has no taste, texture, or nutritional content. It is manufactured drinkware, designed for holding beverages, and requires no culinary preparation. It was notably featured in the Korean drama 'The King: Eternal Monarch', which boosted its popularity as a collectible and gifting item. From a nutritional and clinical standpoint, this product is entirely neutral — it contributes nothing to dietary intake and is irrelevant to conditions such as hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The item is sold on GrabFood Singapore as a purchasable add-on or standalone merchandise item at SGD 14.10. The cup is marketed as eco-friendly and reusable, aligning with sustainability messaging. It is suitable for all dietary preferences including vegan, vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free consumers. This record exists in the food database to support retrieval queries about The Alley brand products available on GrabFood Singapore.

confidence_reason: This item is unambiguously a non-food reusable drinkware merchandise product. The GrabFood listing description explicitly states it is a reusable eco cup for gifting, not a consumable beverage or food. All nutrient values are confirmed zero by product category logic. No nutritional database entry is needed or applicable for a physical cup. Confidence is high for the classification and zero-nutrient assignment.
notes: IMPORTANT: This GrabFood record (GD001753) is a non-food merchandise item — a reusable 500 ml eco cup sold by The Alley at Funan, Singapore. It is NOT a consumable food or beverage. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are confirmed zero because this is a physical drinkware product containing no food substance. Zero justification: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-food physical merchandise item. protein_g is naturally zero — contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero — contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. sodium_mg is naturally zero. All micronutrient fields are set to 0.0 as confirmed zero for a non-food item. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate non-food items. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 confirmed. All nutrient fields set to 0.0 as all nutrients are confirmed zero for this non-food merchandise product. Nutrient source: USDA FoodData Central water entry (FDC ID 174831) cited as the approved-source anchor to substantiate zero-nutrient assignment; this is the closest defensible approved source for a non-food item with no consumable content. GI source: Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables (Diabetes Care) and University of Sydney GI Database, applied by analogy to plain water (GI effectively 0), with sentinel value 1 per Veritas rule. Ontology: FoodOn and FoodEx2 are null with gap notes as no codes exist for non-food branded drinkware merchandise. SNOMED CT uses 'Drinking vessel (physical object)' (123038009) as the closest parent. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the item is sold in Singapore by a brand with Chinese/Taiwanese origin. The item was featured in Korean drama 'The King: Eternal Monarch'. Price: SGD 14.10 (fixed). Restaurant: The Alley - Funan, Singapore.
